_meta.purpose: Curated, structured dose ceilings and high-alert flags for a SMALL set of high-risk drugs. Numeric ceilings are facts drawn from public FDA labeling (openFDA/DailyMed) and are not copyrightable. This file is intentionally short and hand-verified; the long tail is handled by label-text retrieval, not by this file.
_meta.disclaimer: Ceilings are label-derived reference points, not institutional protocol. Verify against current policy and the product label. Not for use as the sole basis of administration.

high_alert_classes: concentrated electrolytes (KCl, hypertonic NaCl, magnesium sulfate)
vasoactive / inotropic infusions (norepinephrine, epinephrine, dopamine, etc.)

acetaminophen.note: OTC labeling commonly caps at 3000 mg/day; some inpatient protocols allow 4000 mg/day. Confirm against local policy.

heparin.note: Weight-based dosing per institutional nomogram. Do not rely on a generic ceiling; use the ordering protocol.

insulin.note: Units only. Never abbreviate 'U'. Verify concentration (U-100 vs U-500) explicitly.

potassium chloride.note: Higher concentrations/rates require central line and monitoring per policy. Never IV push.
